title: Welcome to Paradise (Miami FPV Freestyle) - Johnny FPV 2018
creator: Johnny FPV (Johnny Schaer)
award_or_showcase_provenance: Featured on AirVuz.com (an independent FPV/drone-video showcase platform) with an AirVuz 'Staff Picks' badge, where staff copy describes Johnny FPV as 'the almost undisputed master of cinematic FPV flight'; this is an editorial curation signal, not a juried festival/contest placement like this pack's NY Drone Film Festival or SkyPixel exemplars.
notes: CANDIDATE-SOURCED, INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED THIS SESSION -- one exemplar added to the FPV-freestyle/urban-coastal subgenre bucket. The candidate (title 'Welcome to Paradise (Miami FPV Freestyle) - Johnny FPV 2018', creator 'Johnny FPV (Johnny Schaer)', source_url https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JAWXrtIfY9o) was NOT trusted verbatim -- it was independently re-verified this session via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (WebFetch of https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JAWXrtIfY9o&format=json), which returned title='Welcome to Paradise (Miami FPV Freestyle) - Johnny FPV 2018', author_name='Johnny FPV', author_url='https://www.youtube.com/@JohnnyFPV', provider_name='YouTube' -- an exact field-for-field match to the candidate's title/creator claims. Two independent WebSearches this session corroborated the video's real-world context: the first ('"Welcome to Paradise" Johnny FPV 2018 Miami freestyle youtube') returned an upload date of 2018-01-03 and a description of the video (ocean/beach shots, high-rise hotels to remote lighthouses) matching the title's Miami-freestyle framing, plus corroboration from a third-party listing on AirVuz (an independent FPV/drone-video showcase site: https://www.airvuz.com/video/Welcome-to-Paradise-Miami---Johnny-FPV-2018?id=5a4d41687d6ae161d7e220d7). WebFetch of that AirVuz page this session independently confirmed the same title/creator pairing, an AirVuz 'Staff Picks' badge, and staff copy describing the creator as 'the almost undisputed master of cinematic FPV flight' showcasing Miami's beaches, high-rise dives, yacht chases, and golden-hour shots with a racing drone -- corroborating rather than merely repeating the oEmbed result. The second WebSearch ('Johnny FPV Johnny Schaer Miami drone pilot freestyle') independently corroborated that 'Johnny FPV' is the public alias of Johnny Schaer, a real, widely-documented FPV pilot: a Chicago-based pilot who was one of the first competitors in the Drone Racing League (launched 2015) and has since done cinematography work referenced in coverage tied to brands including Red Bull, Nike, and Mercedes-Benz (this general career context is corroborated by search-result summaries of third-party profile coverage, not independently verified against each named brand's own materials in this session). A third WebFetch (uavcoach.com/johnny-fpv/, an FPV-industry blog) corroborated the same video by name and added disclosed hardware detail not otherwise available: drone = AstroX X5 Freestyle Frame (Johnny FPV V2 edition), camera = GoPro Hero7 Black, FPV camera = RunCam Swift Mini 2 (JohnnyFPV Edition), music = 'Silence' by Marshmello ft. Khalid -- consistent, non-contradictory detail across three independent sources (YouTube oEmbed, AirVuz, uavcoach.com), so this candidate is treated as real and verifiable, not fabricated. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': a direct WebFetch of the YouTube watch page itself (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JAWXrtIfY9o), specifically asked to search the fetched markup for a schema.org VideoObject block, a duration meta/itemprop tag, an ISO-8601 duration string, or any 'Creative Commons'/'CC BY'/license-type text, returned only YouTube's generic footer/navigation/copyright content ('© 2026 Google LLC') with none of those signals present -- no explicit Creative Commons marking was found anywhere for this specific upload. Per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its explicit prohibition on bulk-downloading all-rights-reserved footage. `platform` is confirmed 'youtube' (re-verified, not merely carried over from the discovery-stage claim): the source_url is a standard youtube.com/watch URL, and the oEmbed response's provider_name field independently returned 'YouTube'; this is not an Instagram URL, so the Instagram-specific platform='other'/local_media_path=null-forever rule does not apply here (though local_media_path is still null under the separate all-rights-reserved rule above). `duration` is left null: neither the oEmbed response (which does not carry a duration field) nor the WebFetch of the watch page (which returned no schema.org VideoObject, duration meta tag, or ISO-8601 duration string, per the license-check pass described above) yielded a runtime, and no YouTube Data API call was made in this session -- rather than estimate a plausible length for a freestyle FPV edit (typically 2-5 minutes by genre convention), this field stays unset pending a real API call or manual playback confirmation (see reference_pack/README.md's refresh/expiry process). `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint. Calibration reasoning: sharpness=0.78 sits mid-pack (matching bbc-earth-aerial.json's 0.78) rather than at this pack's top tier (matthiasdangl-maldives-overwater-lagoon-fpv.json's 0.79, dronestagram-feature.json's 0.85) -- the disclosed camera/FPV-cam combination (GoPro Hero7 Black for high-res capture, RunCam Swift Mini 2 analog FPV cam for flying view) is a genuine, disclosed 2018-era hardware baseline, but is older/lower-resolution than the DJI Avata 2/O3-class digital-FPV systems disclosed for some of this pack's more recent exemplars, so it is not scored at the pack's ceiling. exposure=0.77 reflects the AirVuz description's 'golden hour paradise' framing (generally favorable warm daylight over ocean/beach/high-rise subjects, comparable to lido-wave-nazare-coastal.json's coastal daylight case) without a confirmed HDR/log profile or resolution for this exact upload, so it is scored just above this pack's mid-band rather than at its top. motion_smoothness=0.84 is scored near this pack's high end, above sharpness/exposure: this is a deliberate, disclosed reputational signal, not a frame-level measurement -- three independent sources this session (YouTube itself via the video's own framing, AirVuz's staff description as 'the almost undisputed master of cinematic FPV flight', and corroborated career context as one of the Drone Racing League's original 2015 competitive pilots) converge on Johnny FPV/Johnny Schaer being a top-tier, widely-recognized proximity-flying specialist, a stronger and more independently corroborated piloting-reputation signal than several other FPV exemplars in this pack (e.g. flaviufpv-madeira-fanal-forest-fpv.json's 0.74, which had no comparable reputation corroboration) though slightly below matthiasdangl-maldives-overwater-lagoon-fpv.json's 0.85, whose single continuous take was independently confirmed via live playback rather than reputation alone. `composite_score` IS a real computation, not hand-computed: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.78, exposure=0.77, motion_smoothness=0.84, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights(weights_version='default-v1')` (equal 1/3 weighting on sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness, composition weight fixed at 0.0) via `.venv/bin/python -c "..."` in this session, which printed weights=ScoringWeights(weights_version='default-v1', sharpness=0.3333333333333333, exposure=0.3333333333333333, motion_smoothness=0.3333333333333333, composition=0.0) and composite_score=0.7966666666666666 -- because composition is None, compute_composite_score's defensive None-skipping behavior excludes composition from both the weighted sum and the renormalization denominator, reducing the result to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals: (0.78+0.77+0.84)/3 = 0.7966666666666666 (rounded to 0.7967 here), confirmed by actually running the function this session rather than hand-computed.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: format='long-form-cinematic' is a deliberately grounded, structural determination, not a repetition of this candidate's own discovery-stage framing: this session's own oEmbed fetch returned embed dimensions width=200/height=113 -- a landscape 16:9 aspect ratio, NOT the portrait/vertical aspect this pack's genuinely-verified 'short-form-reel' exemplars carry (e.g. pshepfpv-iceland-glaciers-fpv.json's width=113/height=200) -- and the source_url itself is a standard /watch?v= path, not a /shorts/ path or an Instagram Reel URL, the two structural facts this pack consistently uses elsewhere to justify 'short-form-reel' (see e.g. flaviufpv-madeira-fanal-forest-fpv.json, instagram-elisacappelletti-monument-valley.json). So although this candidate was flagged for discovery on the stated basis that Johnny Schaer's proximity-flying clips are widely re-clipped and reposted by third-party Instagram/TikTok compilation accounts, that discovery rationale describes how OTHER accounts have repackaged fragments of his work elsewhere -- it is not evidence about the structural format of this specific canonical YouTube upload, which this session's own verification instead shows to be a standard landscape, non-Shorts YouTube video. Per this schema's honesty contract, the structural verification (landscape, /watch URL) outweighs the unverified secondary-repost claim, so format is set to 'long-form-cinematic' rather than 'short-form-reel'. Separately, and consistent with the FPV-freestyle genre and this pack's other Johnny-FPV-adjacent exemplars (drone-racing-league-waterfall-fpv-dive.json, pshepfpv-iceland-glaciers-fpv.json), the internal editing PACE is understood by genre convention to be fast-cut and music-synchronized (uavcoach.com corroborated the specific soundtrack, 'Silence' by Marshmello ft. Khalid) -- proximity FPV freestyle edits typically string together many short (sub-5-second) shots timed to musical beats, distinct from a slow observational cinematic pace -- but no live playback or scrubbing of this video was performed in this session (no browser-automation tool was invoked), and no text source found described this specific video's cut count, exact shot lengths, or transition types, so `estimated_cut_count`, `avg_shot_length_seconds`, and `transition_styles_observed` all stay null/empty rather than guessed. `review_method` is set to 'text_provenance_only' because every editorial-style inference here rests on oEmbed structural metadata, URL-path structure, and third-party text coverage (AirVuz, uavcoach.com) -- not on an actual playback/scrub of the video.
